india_trends.title: Adani Ports to Adani Power: Adani Group stocks advance after reports of US to settle fraud lawsuit against Gautam Adani
india_trends.summary: On May 15, shares of Adani companies saw gains amid news that US authorities might dismiss fraud charges against Gautam Adani. Adani plans to invest $10 billion in the US, generating around 15,000 jobs, while settling a civil fraud lawsuit.

State-run fuel retailers had absorbed costs but are now passing them on. The increase reflects higher international crude c

india_trends.title: HPCL, BPCL to IOCL: OMC stocks decline up to 2.6% even after petrol, diesel prices raised by ₹3/litre; here's why
india_trends.summary: Shares of state-run oil marketing companies fell despite a petrol and diesel price hike, as investor sentiment remained weak due to lower-than-expected increases and rising global crude prices. Petrol in Delhi is now ₹97.77 per litre, while diesel is ₹90.67 per litre.

india_trends.summary: Gold and silver prices opened sharply lower on MCX Friday, driven by rising energy prices fueling inflation concerns and expectations of prolonged high interest rates. Investors are closely watching U.S.-China trade talks, with gold and silver futures experiencing significant dips. Spot gold and sil

india_trends.title: Alphabet sells yen bonds worth $3.6 billion, largest such issue by foreign company
india_trends.summary: Alphabet has issued a record-breaking 576.5 billion yen ($3.6 billion) in yen-denominated bonds, marking its first such debt offering. This move supports its substantial AI investment program and funding diversification efforts. The issuance saw strong demand from investors, surpassing Berkshire Hat

india_trends.title: Why mid-sized IT firms are more confident about AI than Infosys and HCLTech
india_trends.summary: Smaller IT services firms say nimbleness and domain depth shield them from AI disruption, even as India’s largest outsourcers warn of deflationary impact and rising competitive intensity.

india_trends.title: Rupee opens 11 paise lower at 95.87 against the US dollar
india_trends.summary: The Indian rupee opened weaker at 95.87 against the US dollar, continuing a downward trend due to rising US Treasury yields and high crude oil prices. The rupee has fallen to record lows for three consecutive sessions, influenced by foreign equity withdrawals and high demand for dollars.

india_trends.title: Gold price today: Rate drops on MCX amid a stronger US dollar, higher oil prices; experts highlight key levels to watch
india_trends.summary: Gold price today: Gold rates dropped on the MCX on Friday morning amid a stronger US dollar, driven by elevated crude oil prices. International gold prices declined to an over one-week low, looking set for a weekly decline.

india_trends.title: Silver rate today: MCX silver price falls 4% to ₹2.79 lk/kg on firm dollar, crude price rise; Trump-Xi meeting awaited
india_trends.summary: Silver rate today: Selling intensified across bullion counters, with MCX silver prices plunging ₹11,644, or 4%, to ₹2,79,458 per kg. Gold prices also fell, as MCX gold fell ₹1,623, or 1%, to ₹1,60,355 per 10 grams.

india_trends.title: HPCL, BPCL, IOC shares in focus as petrol, diesel prices hiked by Rs 3/litre
india_trends.summary: Oil marketing companies have raised petrol and diesel prices by up to Rs 3 per litre due to prolonged disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz and escalating global energy crisis. This move aims to alleviate financial pressure on OMCs, which have been absorbing losses amid soaring crude oil prices.
